title: podman-systemd [Build] Volume= documentation bug
body:
### Issue Description

[Manual](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#id55) says

> Mount a volume to containers when executing RUN instructions during the build. This is equivalent to the --volume option of podman build, and generally has the form [[SOURCE-VOLUME|HOST-DIR:]CONTAINER-DIR[:OPTIONS]].

> If SOURCE-VOLUME starts with ., Quadlet resolves the path relative to the location of the unit file.

> Special case:

> If SOURCE-VOLUME ends with .volume, Quadlet will look for the corresponding .volume Quadlet unit. If found, Quadlet will use the name of the Volume set in the Unit, otherwise, systemd-$name is used. The generated systemd service contains a dependency on the service unit generated for that .volume unit, or on $name-volume.service if the .volume unit is not found. Note: the corresponding .volume file must exist.

However, [podman-build manual](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#volume-v-host-dir-container-dir-options) says (and in reality this is how it works) that only HOST-DIR is supported, not referencing a SOURCE-VOLUME.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman create volume test
2. Reference volume test in build ([Build] Volume=test:/test)


### Describe the results you received

systemctl start of the build unit fails

### Describe the results you expected

Docs should not describe referencing SOURCE-VOLUME

### podman info output

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Are you sure you are not misreading the docs? I read:

> If SOURCE-VOLUME ends with .volume, Quadlet will look for the corresponding .volume Quadlet unit

Which I read to mean you need to create a `.volume` file (instead of using `podman volume create`) and specify `Volume=test.volume:/test`. Haven't tested this, but this works for me with the `[Container] Volume=` option, have not tried building.

@matthijskooijman this is about using in build (`[Build] Volume=`), not run (`[Container] Volume=`). `podman build` simply does not support mounting a volume, only host-dir (at least according to my testing back then and its current manual).

> @matthijskooijman this is about using in build ([Build] Volume=), not run ([Container] Volume=). podman build simply does not support mounting a volume, only host-dir (at least according to my testing back then and its current manual).

Yes, I realize that, I'm just saying that the documented syntax for both options is the same, and in your original report you mention a config you tried (`[Build] Volume=test:/test)`) that does not adhere to that syntax. So I'm just suggesting that maybe it is supported, but your test is wrong (or maybe you also tested the right syntax, but just did not write it down in your issue report). But I have not tested this myself, so it's just a suggestion.
